profile.name: Mapping Agent
profile.description: This agent creates visual outputs from prepared datasets, including maps and charts, and writes the result as a PNG image. It chooses an appropriate visualization style from the task and available data structure. It is most useful at the final presentation stage of a workflow when analysis results need to be communicated clearly.

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: Data Visualization Generation
skills.description: Automatically decides between maps and charts based on schema (e.g., presence of geometry), determines z-order, generates Python code to plot the data, and returns the rendered artifact (PNG).

constraints.spatial: Dependent on the Coordinate Reference System (CRS) and geometries present in the provided input datasets.
other: Code execution is strictly limited to a 60-second timeout.
The default workflow is LLM-backed cartographic code generation for polished static maps and charts.
Agent will attempt to fix code errors up to the configured max_iterations value before falling back to a deterministic renderer.
The deterministic renderer is also available as an explicit quick-mapping option.
Agent is strictly forbidden from downloading data from external sources during execution.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits a natural-language geospatial task to the agent. The task.mode field controls sync, async, or stream execution.

input_datasets.description: Required array of input datasets. Each item may be an HTTP/HTTPS URL, a server-accessible path, or a base64-encoded file object with filename, data, and optional mime_type.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response: 'URL' (default) or 'Encoded'.

primary_artifacts.description: Static map, chart, or visualization image generated from input datasets.

model.description: Optional model override. If omitted, the agent uses its default model.

renderer.description: Optional renderer selection. By default, the agent uses the LLM-backed cartographic workflow. Use 'deterministic', 'quick', or 'fast' only when a quick built-in map/chart renderer is preferred over polished LLM-generated cartography.

quick_mapping.description: Optional boolean. If true, the agent uses the deterministic quick-mapping renderer when the input is suitable.

force_llm.description: Optional boolean. If true, the agent skips the deterministic quick renderer and uses the LLM-backed workflow.

max_iterations.description: Optional integer controlling the maximum number of LLM code-generation repair attempts. Values are constrained by the server implementation.

requested_skill.description: Advanced optional skill-selection hint. Most clients should omit this.

credentials.description: Provide one model execution credential in the request credentials object.

provenance.description: Includes llm_calls, tool_calls, token usage (input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens), duration, and number_of_artifacts in the response provenance section.

reproducibility.description: Returns the exact Python script used for execution in the execution.code.script field, along with environment details (Python version, domain-specific libraries).

validation.description: Agent checks code execution success but does not validate image quality or correctness.

access_control.description: Allowed roles set to ['all'] via the extension profile. No role-based access control is enforced.

operation_policy.description: Python code execution sandbox strictly times out after 60 seconds. The default workflow uses LLM-backed cartographic code generation. The deterministic renderer is opt-in through request parameters or explicit quick-mapping instructions, and is also used as a fallback if LLM-generated visualization code repeatedly fails. Agent is forbidden from downloading external data during execution.

review_policy.description: output_review_required is set to false. No human-in-the-loop validation is required.

governance.policy_notes: If the agent is used, please cite the paper on the visualization agent.

extensions.description: This agent uses matplotlib, geopandas, pandas, and mapclassify to generate visual outputs. It is designed for geospatial data visualization tasks and can handle various input formats to produce maps and charts. By default, it uses an LLM-backed cartographic workflow for more polished static maps and charts. A deterministic quick renderer is available when requested through natural-language instructions such as 'quick map' or through parameters such as renderer='deterministic' or quick_mapping=true. Supports classified (choropleth) and unclassified (uniform color) map styles, as well as bar and line charts.
